bill.short_title: Further Continuing Appropriations and Other Extensions Act, 2024

provisions.rate: at a rate for operations as provided in the applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2023 (per P.L. 118-15 as amended)
provisions.duration: through February 2, 2024 (general); through January 19, 2024 (for sections 101(1), 101(4), 101(10), 101(12), 134, and 137)
provisions.raw_text: by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''February 2, 2024, except as provided in paragraph (4)''
notes: SEC. 101 paragraphs (1) and (2) of H.R. 6363
Amends P.L. 118-15 to extend the CR from its prior expiration date
Creates a laddered CR: sections 101(1), 101(4), 101(10), 101(12), 134, and 137 expire January 19, 2024; all other provisions expire February 2, 2024
Paragraph (1) modifies section 106(3); paragraph (2) adds new section 106(4)

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 106
cross_references.description: Modifies CR expiration dates
FY2023 appropriations acts

provisions.description: Modifies section 101(1) of P.L. 118-15 to apply the Rural Water and Waste Disposal Program Account language by inserting 'direct loans and,' before 'loan guarantees', thereby broadening the program to include direct loans during the CR period.
provisions.raw_text: on section 101(1) by inserting after ''except section 729,'' the following: ''and except that the language under the heading 'Rural Utilities Service
notes: SEC. 101 paragraph (3) of H.R. 6363
CR anomaly expanding program authority to include direct loans in addition to loan guarantees
Applies to title III language of the underlying appropriations act

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 101(1)
cross_references.description: Adds direct loan authority to Rural Water program

provisions.description: Modifies section 101(10) of P.L. 118-15 to except section 123 and provisions carrying the same restriction in prior Acts from being continued under the CR.
provisions.raw_text: on section 101(10), by inserting before the period '', except section 123 as well as the provisions carrying the same restriction in prior Acts''
notes: SEC. 101 paragraph (4) of H.R. 6363
Exempts section 123 and equivalent provisions in prior acts from continuation under the CR base text

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 101(10)

provisions.description: Modifies section 123 of P.L. 118-15 by changing the notification/restriction period from 17 days to 94 days and '17-day' to '94-day', adjusting for the extended CR duration.
provisions.raw_text: on section 123, by striking ''17 days'' and inserting ''94 days'' and by striking ''17-day'' and inserting ''94-day''
notes: SEC. 101 paragraph (5) of H.R. 6363
Adjusts timeframe in section 123 of the original CR to reflect the longer CR duration

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 123
cross_references.description: Changes notification/restriction period

provisions.raw_text: on section 124, by striking ''$621,270,000'' and inserting ''$663,070,000''
notes: SEC. 101 paragraph (6) of H.R. 6363
Increases the CR anomaly amount in section 124 of P.L. 118-15 by $41,800,000
Section 124 of P.L. 118-15 was an anomaly providing additional funding above the CR rate for the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
Account name not explicitly stated in H.R. 6363 text; inferred from referenced section of P.L. 118-15

provisions.description: Modifies section 125 of P.L. 118-15 to apply 'through the date specified in section 106(3) of this Act' instead of 'during the period covered by this Act', ensuring the provision extends through the new CR end date.

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 125
cross_references.description: Clarifies duration of provision

notes: Added to P.L. 118-15 by SEC. 101(8) of H.R. 6363

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from: (a) both PAYGO scorecards under the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010; (b) Senate PAYGO scorecards under H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress); and (c) budget scorekeeping for purposes of section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, section 302(a) allocations under the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, and classification as an appropriation Act under the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 701. 
Budgetary Effects
(a) 
Statutory PAYGO Scorecards
The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
notes: Standard budget enforcement exemption provision for non-appropriations divisions of CR/omnibus bills
Subsection (a) exempts from Statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of P.L. 111-139
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards under H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress) section 4106

Subsection (c) exempts from BCA section 251 discretionary caps, CBA section 302(a) allocations, and Statutory PAYGO classification as appropriation Act, notwithstanding Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines from Conference Report 105-217 and section 250(c)(8) of the BCA

cross_references.target: Rule 3, Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines, Conference Report 105-217
cross_references.description: Overrides budget scorekeeping classification rules

cross_references.target: Section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
cross_references.description: Exempts division from statutory PAYGO scorecards

cross_references.target: Section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
cross_references.description: Exempts division from Senate PAYGO scorecards

cross_references.target: Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
cross_references.description: Exempts from discretionary spending limits

cross_references.target: Section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974
cross_references.description: Exempts from committee allocations

cross_references.target: Section 3, paragraph (4)(C) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
cross_references.description: Exempts from classification as appropriation Act

flagged_issues: Bill uses a laddered CR with two expiration dates: January 19, 2024 (for Agriculture, Interior, Military Construction-VA, Transportation-HUD, and certain other provisions under sections 101(1), 101(4), 101(10), 101(12), 134, 137) and February 2, 2024 (all other provisions)
SEC. 101(6) modifies section 124 of P.L. 118-15 which is likely the WIC anomaly; account name not explicitly stated in H.R. 6363 text
SEC. 140 Columbia Class Submarine apportionment ceiling of $3,338,413,000 overrides normal CR rate restrictions
SEC. 145(b) increases transfer authority to Student Aid Administration from 3% to 10%
SEC. 102(a) provides a blanket extension of all Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 authorities — individual programs are also separately extended with specific amendments in subsections (c) and (d)
SEC. 102(d)(9) carves out conservation program mandatory funding (Section 1241(a)(1)(A) and (B)) and supplemental disaster assistance (Section 1614(c)(4)) from the general extension
SEC. 102(g) retroactive effective date excludes the rescission and two research provisions from retroactive application

Total new mandatory spending from specific dollar amounts: $77,500,000 (across 10 provisions with explicit amounts); additional mandatory spending from blanket extension at prior-year rates is not quantified in the bill text
All provisions in this title are mandatory spending extensions or policy provisions; no discretionary appropriations are present
SEC. 302 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund by $561,000,000 (from $6,357,117,810 to $5,796,117,810) as a budget offset
SEC. 503 increases Medicare Improvement Fund by $286,795,056 (from $180,000,000 to $466,795,056)
SEC. 401 contains an indefinite appropriation ('such sums as may be necessary') with no specified dollar amount
SEC. 402 and SEC. 403 fund programs at pro rata FY2023 rates with no explicit dollar amounts
